# Handoff

Start from [./](./). This session resumed the feedback-ring activation track anchored by [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) and pushed three production edges forward: frontend ingest was already wired earlier in the session, then the stop-hook path was extended so transcript capture now mines rule-confusion feedback and can synthesize a follow-up ticket, and the validation CLI now triggers best-effort spec verified-state recomputation after execution records land. The remaining blocker is not code compilation; it is evidence and regeneration: root-generated guidance did not refresh because `cargo run -p rule-cli --bin rule -- sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml` fails with a path-resolution/storage error, and no live `.test` evidence has been recorded yet for the newly wired paths.

## Current Working Directory

- Use [./](./) as the starting directory.
- Treat the worktree as noisy: root has many unrelated untracked [./.ticket](./.ticket) entries plus new [./.feedback](./.feedback) and [./.session](./.session) artifacts. Do not clean or revert unrelated ticket artifacts without confirming ownership.

## Long-Horizon Goal

- Make the feedback system self-driving in production, not just compiled and unit-tested: discovery is documented and reachable, collection ingests live signals, and analyzer logic synthesizes at least one real action from stored feedback.
- The main execution ticket for this session is [6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc [feedback-api][activation] Wire feedback discovery, collection, and analyzer loop (ring activation)](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/ticket.toml).
- The broader tracker is [5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d [content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring — execution→verified, transcript mining, missing-rule tickets, ticket-entity feedback](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/ticket.toml).

## What Was Done

- Frontend ingest had already been wired earlier in the session by mounting feedback HTTP routes into ticket-viewer and adding ticket-detail feedback actions in [./memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/src/main.rs](./memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/src/main.rs), [./memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/frontend/dioxus/src/api.rs](./memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/frontend/dioxus/src/api.rs), [./memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/frontend/dioxus/src/components/ticket_detail/actions.rs](./memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/frontend/dioxus/src/components/ticket_detail/actions.rs), [./memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/frontend/dioxus/src/components/ticket_detail/page.rs](./memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/frontend/dioxus/src/components/ticket_detail/page.rs), and [./memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/frontend/dioxus/src/components/ticket_detail/ui/render.rs](./memory-viewers/ticket-viewer/frontend/dioxus/src/components/ticket_detail/ui/render.rs).
- Stop-hook orchestration was extended in [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs) so the hook now captures the session, loads rule bodies, runs transcript mining with `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion`, and synthesizes a tracker-improvement ticket when mined rule-confusion feedback exists and no matching analyzer-created ticket title already exists.
- New dependencies for that hook slice were added in [./memory-api/crates/session-api/Cargo.toml](./memory-api/crates/session-api/Cargo.toml).
- The execution→verified ring edge was activated in [./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/lib.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/lib.rs) and [./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs): after `test record` and `test run`, linked spec ids are now passed through `spec-api` verified-state recomputation on a best-effort basis.
- The `spec-api` dependency for that CLI path was added in [./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/Cargo.toml](./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/Cargo.toml).
- Discovery wiring was updated by registering `feedback-mcp` in [./.github/mcp.json](./.github/mcp.json) and [./.vscode/mcp.json](./.vscode/mcp.json).
- The shared feedback workflow guidance was rewritten at [./memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f-71fe-4b9e-9a7f-66fbd262f631/body.md](./memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f-71fe-4b9e-9a7f-66fbd262f631/body.md) so it points at the feedback-api transports (`feedback ingest/inbox/summary/mine` and `feedback_ingest/feedback_inbox/feedback_query/feedback_summary/feedback_mine`) instead of the old rule-only feedback path.
- The missing root dependency edge was added from [5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d [content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring — execution→verified, transcript mining, missing-rule tickets, ticket-entity feedback](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/ticket.toml) to [8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8 [program][multi-store] Store expansion and operational health program](./.ticket/tickets/8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8/ticket.toml).

## Validated Results

- `cargo check --manifest-path memory-api/crates/session-api/Cargo.toml` passed after the stop-hook mining/analyzer changes.
- `cargo check -p test-cli` passed after wiring execution recording to spec verified-state recomputation.
- `cargo check -p feedback-mcp` passed after adding MCP discovery entries.
- A direct JSON parse check succeeded for [./.github/mcp.json](./.github/mcp.json) and [./.vscode/mcp.json](./.vscode/mcp.json).
- `cargo run -p ticket-cli -- --workspace . link --from 5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d --to 8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8 --kind depends_on --reason "feedback-ring activation depends on the execution-to-verified edge ticket" --toon` succeeded.
- Earlier in the same session, before this handoff slice, `cargo check -p ticket-viewer` and the Dioxus wasm check passed for the frontend-ingest changes.

## Findings And Decisions

- The original ticket assumption that `feedback-mcp`, `feedback-http`, and `feedback-cli` were missing was false. Those transports already existed; the real missing work was registration and live callers.
- The smallest production host for transcript mining was the capture hook, not a new daemon. The controlling path was [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs), because it already persisted the just-ended session and had immediate access to the captured turns.
- The smallest production host for execution→verified was `test-cli` right after recording executions, not a wider redesign of test transport layers.
- The shared feedback workflow rule owner is not a root editable generated file; the durable rule text changed in [./memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f-71fe-4b9e-9a7f-66fbd262f631/body.md](./memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f-71fe-4b9e-9a7f-66fbd262f631/body.md). Root-generated guidance still needs regeneration once the root rule-sync blocker is fixed.
- The code slices now compile, but acceptance is still incomplete because there is no recorded `.test` evidence for live edge firings from the newly activated production paths.

## Blockers

- Root rule regeneration is blocked. `cargo run -p rule-cli --bin rule -- sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml` fails from repo root with `storage error: io error: Das System kann den angegebenen Pfad nicht finden. (os error 3)`. This prevented refresh of root-generated guidance such as [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md).
- A narrower regeneration did succeed: `cargo run -p rule-cli --bin rule -- sync-targets --config memory-viewers/rule-targets.yaml`. That confirms the shared rule body is syntactically fine, but it does not update root-generated artifacts.
- Board ownership is stale. The active session’s old lease on [6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc [feedback-api][activation] Wire feedback discovery, collection, and analyzer loop (ring activation)](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/ticket.toml) is stale and still claims several ticket-viewer files. Another stale lease exists on [5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d [content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring — execution→verified, transcript mining, missing-rule tickets, ticket-entity feedback](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/ticket.toml). Reconcile board state before further edits, especially if touching `crates/feedback-api` or `crates/rule-api/src`.
- No browser/manual proof and no `.test` execution records exist yet for the newly wired stop-hook or execution→verified paths.

## Next Actions In Order

1. Reconcile board state for [6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc [feedback-api][activation] Wire feedback discovery, collection, and analyzer loop (ring activation)](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/ticket.toml) in the `memory-api` workspace.
Reason: the stale lease can block legitimate file ownership and hides whether this session is still the active owner.
First validation check: `ticket board show --workspace c:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api --toon` or the equivalent MCP board read should show a non-stale current owner after cleanup and re-check-in.

2. Debug and fix the root rule regeneration failure, then rerun `cargo run -p rule-cli --bin rule -- sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml`.
Reason: the shared feedback workflow rule body is updated, but root-generated artifacts such as [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) are still stale until the root sync path works.
First validation check: the root sync command exits successfully and [./AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md) reflects feedback-api guidance instead of the old rule-only path.

3. Exercise the stop-hook path end-to-end using a real or controlled session transcript so the new hook actually mines feedback and, if applicable, creates a follow-up ticket.
Reason: compile success is not acceptance; the activation ticket requires a live production firing with evidence.
First validation check: inspect [./.session/sessions/3b33ae8c-6c51-431f-a479-d46952f4f1c0/session.json](./.session/sessions/3b33ae8c-6c51-431f-a479-d46952f4f1c0/session.json), [./.session/sessions/3b33ae8c-6c51-431f-a479-d46952f4f1c0/transcript.json](./.session/sessions/3b33ae8c-6c51-431f-a479-d46952f4f1c0/transcript.json), and [./.feedback](./.feedback) for persisted mined entries and confirm whether a new tracker-improvement ticket was opened.

4. Record `.test` evidence for the execution→verified edge by using `test record` or `test run` with a linked spec whose guard conditions can pass.
Reason: the code now recomputes verified state, but there is no durable validation evidence proving the path fired from production CLI behavior.
First validation check: the execution record output includes `verified_spec_ids` or an explicit `verified_recompute_errors` field, and the linked spec manifest/state changes as expected.

5. Re-run the ticket-viewer/live frontend path and record `.test` or manual evidence for the already-wired feedback ingest edge.
Reason: that slice compiled earlier but still lacks durable evidence for acceptance.
First validation check: feedback submitted from the ticket-viewer UI appears in the feedback store via `feedback summary` or `feedback inbox` for the target ticket URN.

6. Once evidence exists for all reachable edges, update ticket state and summary on [6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc [feedback-api][activation] Wire feedback discovery, collection, and analyzer loop (ring activation)](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/ticket.toml).
Reason: the ticket is still `new` even though substantial implementation landed.
First validation check: the ticket reflects current implementation, blocker, validation, and docs status before moving toward `in-review`.

## Completion Status

- Completed: frontend ingest code path compiles and is wired through ticket-viewer, but still lacks live evidence.
- Completed: stop-hook capture now mines transcript feedback and can synthesize a follow-up ticket, and the `session-api` crate compiles.
- Completed: execution→verified recompute is wired into `test-cli`, and the `test-cli` crate compiles.
- Completed: `feedback-mcp` is registered in [./.github/mcp.json](./.github/mcp.json) and [./.vscode/mcp.json](./.vscode/mcp.json), and the package builds.
- Completed: the root dependency edge from [5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d [content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring — execution→verified, transcript mining, missing-rule tickets, ticket-entity feedback](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/ticket.toml) to [8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8 [program][multi-store] Store expansion and operational health program](./.ticket/tickets/8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8/ticket.toml) is in place.
- In-progress: discovery guidance changed at the shared rule source in [./memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f-71fe-4b9e-9a7f-66fbd262f631/body.md](./memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f-71fe-4b9e-9a7f-66fbd262f631/body.md), but root-generated outputs are blocked on failed root rule sync.
- Blocked: acceptance evidence is incomplete because no `.test` executions or equivalent live proofs have been recorded for the newly activated production edges.

## Key Entities

- [6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc [feedback-api][activation] Wire feedback discovery, collection, and analyzer loop (ring activation)](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/ticket.toml) — main implementation ticket for this session.
- [./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/description.md](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/description.md) — acceptance criteria and the original dead-edge diagnosis.
- [5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d [content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring — execution→verified, transcript mining, missing-rule tickets, ticket-entity feedback](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/ticket.toml) — broader tracker now explicitly linked to the program ticket.
- [8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8 [program][multi-store] Store expansion and operational health program](./.ticket/tickets/8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8/ticket.toml) — root program ticket newly added as a dependency edge target.
- [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs) — controlling path for the transcript-mining production edge.
- [./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs) — controlling path for the execution→verified production edge.
- [./memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f-71fe-4b9e-9a7f-66fbd262f631/body.md](./memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f-71fe-4b9e-9a7f-66fbd262f631/body.md) — shared feedback workflow rule body that now references feedback-api transports.
- [./.github/mcp.json](./.github/mcp.json) and [./.vscode/mcp.json](./.vscode/mcp.json) — repo MCP client configs now registering `feedback-mcp`.
- [./.session/sessions/3b33ae8c-6c51-431f-a479-d46952f4f1c0/session.json](./.session/sessions/3b33ae8c-6c51-431f-a479-d46952f4f1c0/session.json) — persisted stop-hook session artifact for this conversation.
- [./.session/sessions/3b33ae8c-6c51-431f-a479-d46952f4f1c0/transcript.json](./.session/sessions/3b33ae8c-6c51-431f-a479-d46952f4f1c0/transcript.json) — persisted transcript artifact for inspecting what the hook captured.
- [./.session/sessions/3b33ae8c-6c51-431f-a479-d46952f4f1c0/events.json](./.session/sessions/3b33ae8c-6c51-431f-a479-d46952f4f1c0/events.json) — event-level capture details if transcript mining behavior needs debugging.
- [./.rule/README.md](./.rule/README.md) — root rule catalog showing the feedback workflow entry and its intended root ownership, useful while debugging the failed root sync path.

## Board And Persisted Session Notes

- The `memory-api` draftboard currently reports a stale entry for [6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc [feedback-api][activation] Wire feedback discovery, collection, and analyzer loop (ring activation)](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/ticket.toml) with entry id `3d48a016-9086-4d54-9099-b5efd6f78ed6`. It previously claimed several ticket-viewer files. Clean or reclaim it before continuing.
- The board also shows a stale entry for [5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d [content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring — execution→verified, transcript mining, missing-rule tickets, ticket-entity feedback](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/ticket.toml) with agent `copilot-gpt5-codex`, which matters if you need to touch `crates/feedback-api` or `crates/rule-api/src`.
- Persisted session history now exists under [./.session](./.session). Use those artifacts as debugging evidence for the stop-hook path rather than replaying the entire chat transcript.

## Definition Of Done

- Root-generated discovery guidance is refreshed successfully and no longer points agents at the old rule-only feedback workflow.
- `feedback-mcp` is registered and usable from repo MCP configs.
- Live production evidence exists for each intended ring edge that is claimed by the activation ticket, including stop-hook mining and execution→verified recomputation, with recorded `.test` evidence or equivalent durable validation records.
- The analyzer path produces at least one real action from stored feedback and that action is inspectable through the store or ticket system.
- [6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc [feedback-api][activation] Wire feedback discovery, collection, and analyzer loop (ring activation)](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/ticket.toml) is updated with current implementation, validation, and blocker state and can move forward cleanly.

Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

events.reasoning_text:
I'm reviewing the confusion marker logic—it's just a basic keyword bag-of-words approach with a threshold of at least 2 matching tokens, which is pretty crude. I should also note the algorithmic complexity here: it scales with the number of turns, rules, and tokens, which could get expensive.

There's also the issue that every rule overlap in every turn generates a feedback entry, creating potential spam. And I'm noticing the repo has checked-in session transcripts in `.session/events.json`—a 40k-line file that's polluting search results. The grep shows `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion` appearing 56 times across 5 files, mostly inside those session event logs, so the codebase is literally indexing its own chat history.

I have what I need to write this up. I'll lead with the worst offense, order by severity, cite specific file paths and line numbers, and close with a verdict and the highest-impact fix. The overlap between keywords and confusion markers creates a mass false-positive problem — any assistant response mentioning common words like "spec" or "file" triggers feedback across nearly every rule, essentially auto-generating spam tickets. And every signal gets hardcoded as "Mixed" regardless of actual confidence, so the whole rating system is just theater.

data_json.content:
I've read enough to press charges. Here's the roast, ordered by how badly each crime will hurt you in production.

---

## 🔥 The Roast: "Feedback Ring" — a self-driving false-positive factory

You built a machine that reads chat logs, decides the AI was "confused," and then *opens tickets about it automatically*. Let me show you why that's a Rube Goldberg machine bolted to a fire alarm that goes off when someone microwaves popcorn.

---

### Crime #1 — The "analyzer" is `Ctrl+F` cosplaying as machine learning (WORST OFFENDER)

Evidence: [memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L82-L110)

This is the beating heart of the whole "self-driving feedback system," and it's a bag-of-words toy:

- `tokenize` keeps any alphanumeric run ≥ 4 chars ([line 82](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L82)). So "spec", "file", "code", "rule", "ticket", "agent" are all now "meaningful signal."
- `contains_confusion_signal` fires if a turn contains a token *starting with* `error`, `fail`, `wrong`, `conflict`… ([line 90](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L90)). Every normal engineering sentence — "the test failed", "fixed the error" — trips it. Congratulations, you've detected that developers talk about errors.
- The match threshold is a jaw-dropping **`overlap < 2`** ([line 50](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L50)). **Two** shared common words between a turn and a rule body and — *ding* — "likely rule confusion."

So a turn like *"the spec file was unclear and the test failed"* matches basically **every rule that mentions 'spec' or 'file'**, and records a feedback entry for each one. Your "AI-powered rule-confusion analyzer" is `if word_count >= 2 && contains("fail")`. That's not mining, that's panning for gold in a car wash.

And the stopword list "defending" you? [Lines 114-119](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L114-L119) — a hand-rolled list of **nineteen** words. NLTK is weeping. "code", "spec", "must", "rule" sail right through as high-signal keywords.

Cherry on top: every single mined signal is hardcoded to `FeedbackRating::Mixed` ([line 64](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L64)). You built a rating enum and then decided everything is a shrug emoji. Rating theater.

---

### Crime #2 — It writes to disk *inside the detection loop*, then auto-creates tickets

Evidence: [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L67-L76) + [copilot-capture-hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs#L143-L211)

The nested `for turn { for rule { … record_entry(entry)? } }` calls `feedback_store.record_entry` **per turn, per matching rule**, synchronously ([line 68](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L68)). One chatty session with N confusion-ish turns and M rules = a burst of disk writes and a pile of `Mixed`-rated noise, all from a heuristic that thinks "fail" is a smoking gun.

Then the capture hook takes that garbage and **opens tickets from it** ([synthesize_follow_up_tickets, line 144](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs#L144)). Your "at least one real action from stored feedback" success criterion is technically satisfiable by a system that fabricates the action from a false positive it invented four functions ago. That's not closing a feedback ring — that's a snake eating its own tail and filing a ticket about the taste.

The dedup guard? String equality on ticket titles ([line 176](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs#L176)) via a full `ticket_store.list(...)` scan of every `tracker-improvement` ticket, every hook invocation. O(tickets) linear scan on the write path, deduping by exact title match — change one character in the format string and the floodgates reopen.

---

### Crime #3 — "Best-effort" is engineer-speak for "I gave up and put the errors in a Vec"

Evidence: [memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs](memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs#L433-L449)

The execution→verified edge you're so proud of:

```rust
Ok(true)  => verified_spec_ids.push(...),
Ok(false) => {},                          // silent
Err(error) => errors.push(...),           // "best-effort"
```

Recomputation errors get swept into a `verified_recompute_errors` array that rides along in the JSON and is, based on your own handoff, read by *nobody*. You renamed "swallowing failures" to "best-effort on a per-spec basis" and put it in a commit message. Slick. The whole point of a verified-state edge is trust; you built one that fails quietly and hopes someone greps the output later.

---

### Crime #4 — You committed your chat logs and now the repo indexes itself

Evidence: a single grep for `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion` returned **56 matches across 5 files** — and the majority are inside [.session/sessions/](.session) `events.json` / `transcript.json` blobs (e.g. a 40,000+ line `events.json`).

Your own handoff calls the worktree "noisy" with untracked `.ticket`, `.feedback`, and `.session` artifacts. That's not noise, that's a landfill. You've checked in serialized conversation transcripts containing full source diffs, so now every code search in this repo returns your past self talking about the code three times over. The feedback system's raw material is polluting the very searches you'd use to maintain the feedback system. Ouroboros-driven development.

---

### Crime #5 — The regeneration you can't run, blocking the docs you can't refresh

Evidence: handoff Blockers — `rule sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml` dies with `os error 3` ("Das System kann den angegebenen Pfad nicht finden").

The narrower `memory-viewers/rule-targets.yaml` sync works; the root one face-plants on a path it can't resolve. So the durable rule body is updated in exactly one place ([memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f.../body.md](memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f-71fe-4b9e-9a7f-66fbd262f631/body.md)) while [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) still cheerfully points agents at the *old, deleted* feedback path. Your generated-docs pipeline is a single point of failure that fails, and the "source of truth" is now lying to every agent that reads it. A doc generator that can't generate is just a `.gitignore` with extra steps.

---

## Verdict

The plumbing compiles, the transports exist, the ceremony is immaculate — and the intelligence at the center of it is a substring matcher with an `overlap < 2` threshold that will auto-file tickets about phantom "confusion" the moment anyone says the word "fail." You didn't close the feedback ring; you built a perpetual-motion noise machine and wired it directly to your ticket tracker's create endpoint. It's a beautifully-typed Rust cathedral built around a `grep`.

**Highest-leverage fix:** Before *anything* else, gate the analyzer. Raise the signal bar dramatically — TF-IDF or embedding similarity over a hand-rolled 19-word stopword list, a real threshold instead of `overlap < 2`, and stop hardcoding `Mixed`. Most importantly: **feedback mining must not auto-create tickets.** Have it write *proposals* to an inbox a human triages. A self-driving system whose steering wheel is `str::starts_with("fail")` should not have its foot on the ticket-creation accelerator.

And `git rm -r --cached .session/` — stop letting your diary write your search results.

That's not closing a feedback ring — that's a snake eating its own tail and filing a ticket about the taste.

The dedup guard? String equality on ticket titles ([line 176](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs#L176)) via a full `ticket_store.list(...)` scan of every `tracker-improvement` ticket, every hook invocation. O(tickets) linear scan on the write path, deduping by exact title match — change one character in the format string and the floodgates reopen.

---

### Crime #3 — "Best-effort" is engineer-speak for "I gave up and put the errors in a Vec"

Evidence: [memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs](memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs#L433-L449)

The execution→verified edge you're so proud of:

```rust
Ok(true)  => verified_spec_ids.push(...),
Ok(false) => {},                          // silent
Err(error) => errors.push(...),           // "best-effort"
```

Recomputation errors get swept into a `verified_recompute_errors` array that rides along in the JSON and is, based on your own handoff, read by *nobody*. You renamed "swallowing failures" to "best-effort on a per-spec basis" and put it in a commit message. Slick. The whole point of a verified-state edge is trust; you built one that fails quietly and hopes someone greps the output later.

---

### Crime #4 — You committed your chat logs and now the repo indexes itself

Evidence: a single grep for `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion` returned **56 matches across 5 files** — and the majority are inside [.session/sessions/](.session) `events.json` / `transcript.json` blobs (e.g. a 40,000+ line `events.json`).

Your own handoff calls the worktree "noisy" with untracked `.ticket`, `.feedback`, and `.session` artifacts. That's not noise, that's a landfill. You've checked in serialized conversation transcripts containing full source diffs, so now every code search in this repo returns your past self talking about the code three times over. The feedback system's raw material is polluting the very searches you'd use to maintain the feedback system. Ouroboros-driven development.

---

### Crime #5 — The regeneration you can't run, blocking the docs you can't refresh

Evidence: handoff Blockers — `rule sync-targets --config rule-targets.yaml` dies with `os error 3` ("Das System kann den angegebenen Pfad nicht finden").

The narrower `memory-viewers/rule-targets.yaml` sync works; the root one face-plants on a path it can't resolve. So the durable rule body is updated in exactly one place ([memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f.../body.md](memory-viewers/.rule/rules/acd60e9f-71fe-4b9e-9a7f-66fbd262f631/body.md)) while [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) still cheerfully points agents at the *old, deleted* feedback path. Your generated-docs pipeline is a single point of failure that fails, and the "source of truth" is now lying to every agent that reads it. A doc generator that can't generate is just a `.gitignore` with extra steps.

---

## Verdict

The plumbing compiles, the transports exist, the ceremony is immaculate — and the intelligence at the center of it is a substring matcher with an `overlap < 2` threshold that will auto-file tickets about phantom "confusion" the moment anyone says the word "fail." You didn't close the feedback ring; you built a perpetual-motion noise machine and wired it directly to your ticket tracker's create endpoint. It's a beautifully-typed Rust cathedral built around a `grep`.

**Highest-leverage fix:** Before *anything* else, gate the analyzer. Raise the signal bar dramatically — TF-IDF or embedding similarity over a hand-rolled 19-word stopword list, a real threshold instead of `overlap < 2`, and stop hardcoding `Mixed`. Most importantly: **feedback mining must not auto-create tickets.** Have it write *proposals* to an inbox a human triages. A self-driving system whose steering wheel is `str::starts_with("fail")` should not have its foot on the ticket-creation accelerator.

And `git rm -r --cached .session/` — stop letting your diary write your search results.

I'm reviewing the confusion marker logic—it's just a basic keyword bag-of-words approach with a threshold of at least 2 matching tokens, which is pretty crude. I should also note the algorithmic complexity here: it scales with the number of turns, rules, and tokens, which could get expensive.

There's also the issue that every rule overlap in every turn generates a feedback entry, creating potential spam. And I'm noticing the repo has checked-in session transcripts in `.session/events.json`—a 40k-line file that's polluting search results. The grep shows `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion` appearing 56 times across 5 files, mostly inside those session event logs, so the codebase is literally indexing its own chat history.

I have what I need to write this up. I'll lead with the worst offense, order by severity, cite specific file paths and line numbers, and close with a verdict and the highest-impact fix. The overlap between keywords and confusion markers creates a mass false-positive problem — any assistant response mentioning common words like "spec" or "file" triggers feedback across nearly every rule, essentially auto-generating spam tickets. And every signal gets hardcoded as "Mixed" regardless of actual confidence, so the whole rating system is just theater.

1. yes, we should not detect signals from message text yet, only from structured metadata. later we can use smarter embedding techniques to reason about message context for feedback mining
2. yes, we need to iterate the entities in a breadth first order and only queue new entities for detection after each file processed, so entites discovered later are appended to the queue. alternatively we can only mine the entities detected at the beginning.
3. agreed, we should only implement logic that confidently works and has clear and actionable error states, which we need to encode in the type system
4. partially agree - we need to investigate these mined  tickets and why each of them was triggered. auto-detecting confusion was one way of feedback ingestion, and maybe the findings are useful. but we need to understand what these tickets need to be formatted like to become fully backtracable and forward actionable and verifyable. We should not rely on message text language features though, and focus more on failed tool calls or explicit feedback ingestion calls.
5. agree - this should be investigated and fixed

your assessment is generally fair and we need to use a more robust model. prepare to work on everything systematically.

So `.session/sessions/3b33ae8c/...` wouldn't be caught by that one-level pattern at all. And since `!.session/sessions/` re-includes the sessions directory itself, everything nested under it would be tracked by default. The `!` rules are kind of redundant or confused about what they're actually protecting — the real leak is that all those session subfolders with transcripts are being tracked because they're not actually excluded in the first place.

The `.gitignore` is the final piece: it does `.session/*` then re-includes `!.session/sessions/` and `!.session/sessions/default/**` ([.gitignore L36-40](.gitignore#L36)). But `.session/*` only matches **one level**, so every `.session/sessions/<uuid>/…` transcript sits *below* that glob and is never ignored — the `default`-only carve-out is a fiction. That's why 40+ phantom matches drown every search. Decision #5 confirmed, with a real root cause.

I've now grounded all five decisions in concrete evidence. Here's the systematic plan, then I'll reconcile the board and start.

---

## Systematic Plan — "Robust Feedback Model"

### Evidence baseline (what's actually true)
| Decision | Root-cause evidence |
|---|---|
| #1 kill text signals | [transcript_feedback.rs L88-110](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L88) — prefix-match markers + `overlap < 2` bag-of-words |
| #2 BFS queue | current miner is `for turn { for rule { record } }`, no queue, no ordering guarantee |
| #3 typed error states | [verification.rs L52](memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/verification.rs#L52) `Ok(false)` = 3 states collapsed; [handlers.rs L433-449](memory-api/tools/cli/test-cli/src/handlers.rs#L433) swallows errors into `Vec<String>` |
| #4 structured signals + traceable tickets | `SessionTurnEventMeta.tool_success/tool_call_id/tool_arguments_json` exist ([model.rs L54-77](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs#L54)); miner discards `session_id` in provenance ([transcript_feedback.rs L66](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs#L66)); **101 spam tickets already created** |
| #5 repo self-indexing | [.gitignore L36-40](.gitignore#L36) one-level glob leaks all session transcripts |

### Phase 0 — Containment (stop active harm; reversible)
1. **Neutralize the runaway miner** so the next stop-hook fire stops creating spam (gate synthesis behind the not-yet-built structured path).
2. **Fix `.gitignore`** so `.session/sessions/**` transcripts stop polluting search (preserve any deliberate `default` allowlist).
3. **Quarantine the 101 spam tickets** — investigate a sample per your decision #4, then bulk-cancel as auto-generated false positives (I'll confirm before mass-cancel).

### Phase 1 — Structured-metadata mining (decisions #1 + #2)
4. Replace `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion` with a signal extractor that reads **only** structured fields: `tool_success == Some(false)` (failed tool call) and explicit feedback-ingestion tool calls parsed from `tool_arguments_json`. No `content` tokenization.
5. Deterministic **BFS queue**: process turns in sequence, append newly-discovered entities to a work queue, dedupe by URN.

### Phase 2 — Typed outcomes (decision #3)
6. Replace recompute `Result<bool,String>` with an explicit enum (`Verified` / `NotVerifiable{reason}` / `VerificationFailed{failed_guards}` / `Error`) and surface it structurally through `test-cli` instead of the swallow-into-`Vec` pattern.

### Phase 3 — Backtraceable feedback + ticket format (decision #4)
7. Extend `FeedbackProvenance` with `session_id` + turn/tool-call refs so every mined entry backtracks to its origin.
8. Define the backtraceable/forward-actionable/verifiable ticket template driven by structured signals.

---

data_json.reasoningText:
I've got the board reconciled and ownership of the files is confirmed. For Phase 0, the critical move is shutting down the runaway text miner that's generating spam—I need to strip out the text-signal detection as planned, then pivot the miner to work only with structured metadata, which also sets up Phase 1. Though I'm still working through decision #4 about how failed tool calls map to entities and what the ticket format should be.

The immediate harm is coming from the auto-ticket synthesis in the hook that's fed by text mining. I'm going to do the containment surgically and reversibly: replace the text-based mining logic in the transcript feedback module with structured-signal extraction instead. This kills the false positives while advancing Phase 1 at the same time. I need to be careful not to sprawl into a massive multi-file rewrite all at once though—I should validate incrementally as I go.

Looking at what's actually settled versus what still needs investigation: text-signal detection is definitely going, structured signals come from failed tool calls and explicit feedback ingestion, typed error states are locked in, and the gitignore fix is straightforward. But the ticket format itself and how failed tool calls map to entities—that's still open. So I should pause the auto-synthesis of tickets until the format is designed, and focus on getting the structured signal mining into the feed first.

The right move is to start with containment by disabling auto-ticket-synthesis, then swap in the structured-signal miner that records feedback entries with full provenance instead. After that, I'll do the typed recompute, fix the gitignore, and handle the triage of existing tickets. I'll begin with the smallest, safest, most settled changes—gitignore and the containment step—validating as I go.

For the gitignore, the current pattern is too broad and leaks all sessions when the intent is to track only the default one. I need to rewrite it to ignore all session directories except the default, though git's re-inclusion rules after a parent exclusion make this tricky.

The fix is to explicitly ignore all direct children of `.session/`, re-include the `sessions` directory, then ignore all session subdirectories but re-include `default` and its contents. This way, non-default sessions like `3b33ae8c` get caught by the blanket exclusion and their children are blocked too, while `default` and everything under it stays tracked.

Now I need to decide whether to also untrack files that are already committed. Using `git rm --cached` would handle that, but it's a larger operation. I'll apply the `.gitignore` fix first to prevent future tracking, then check the current tracking status with `git status` or `git ls-files` to see if the transcripts are already in the repository and need manual untracking.

The helper function `recompute_linked_specs` returns a tuple of verified spec IDs and errors, and it's invoked at two points in the code—line 92 during record operations and line 366 during run operations—with the actual verification state update happening at line 437. I'm designing a typed outcome enum to cleanly represent the distinct verification states in the spec-api verification module.

I'll introduce a `SpecVerificationOutcome` enum that captures the four possible states currently collapsed into `Ok(false)`: `NotApplicable` when there are no guards, `Pending` when guards are declared but not all have executed, `Failed` when all guards have executed but some didn't pass, and `Verified` when all guards passed and the state gets updated. The `recompute_spec_verified_state` function will return `Result<SpecVerificationOutcome, String>`, keeping the error type as String for now to scope the change—the real ambiguity the user flagged is the collapsed outcome, which this enum resolves, while storage and IO failures can stay as String errors for the moment. Now I need to export `SpecVerificationOutcome` from spec-api's lib.rs and update test-cli's `recompute_linked_specs` handler to return richer typed outcomes instead of just string vectors.

I'm redesigning the return type to be a structured list where each spec gets its own report object containing the spec ID, status, and any relevant details like missing guards, failed guards, or errors—this keeps the JSON output more informative while surfacing the actionable states that Decision #3 requires.

Implementing the function... Iterating through each spec ID to recompute its verification state, then converting the outcome to JSON format with either the result or an error message, and defining a helper function to construct the JSON response object based on the verification outcome...

events.reasoning_text: I've hit a checkpoint where the ticket workflow is progressing smoothly. Now I need to take stock of what's been validated so far—the initial setup and core tracking are solid—but there's a pre-existing issue I've uncovered that needs addressing before I can move forward into the more complex territory around spam handling, transcript ingestion, and schema decisions. I should lay out what's complete, what's broken, and what decisions I'm waiting on from the user.

data_json.content:
Solid checkpoint reached. Here's where things stand.

## Done & validated this session

**Phase 0 — Containment**
- **Killed text-signal mining** (decision #1). Rewrote [transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs) — deleted `tokenize`/`contains_confusion_signal`/`extract_rule_keywords`/`overlap<2`. New `mine_structured_feedback_signals` reads **only** structured metadata (`SessionRole::Tool` + `tool_success == Some(false)`), returns backtraceable `StructuredFeedbackSignal` (carries `tool_call_id`/`event_id`). Side-effect-free.
- **Paused the spam factory** (decision #4). The hook ([copilot-capture-hook.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs)) no longer auto-creates tickets or mines text — it now just logs a structured signal summary. Deleted `synthesize_follow_up_tickets` + all its `feedback_api`/`rule_api`/`ticket_api` machinery.
- **Fixed the `.gitignore` leak** (decision #5) — the one-level `.session/*` glob now has an explicit `.session/sessions/*` exclusion with a `default` carve-out.

**Phase 2 — Typed outcomes (decision #3)**
- Replaced `recompute_spec_verified_state`'s ambiguous `Result<bool, String>` with typed `SpecVerificationOutcome` (`NoGuards` / `Pending{missing_guards}` / `Failed{failed_guards}` / `Verified`) in [verification.rs](memory-api/crates/spec-api/src/verification.rs). test-cli now emits structured per-spec `spec_verification` reports instead of swallowing errors into a `Vec<String>`.

**Validation:** session-api 13/13 green, spec-api verification 3/3 (incl. 2 new typed-outcome tests), spec-api + test-cli compile clean. Durable evidence: `exec-vt-feedback-ring-robust-model-20260713` (passed, linked to the ticket). Board reconciled (24 stale entries cleaned, re-checked-in as `copilot-opus`), ticket moved `new → in-implementation`.

## One thing I discovered (not mine)
`cargo test -p test-cli` has **5 pre-existing failures** — proven red at HEAD. Commit `745d2ef` added the interoperability provenance contract ([test-api/src/lib.rs L314](memory-api/crates/test-api/src/lib.rs#L314)) but never updated test-cli's fixtures to supply `provenance.domain/operation/run_id`. All 5 panic at `record_execution`, entirely upstream of my changes. I deliberately did **not** rewrite that feature's fixtures (scope).

## Decisions I need before proceeding — these are the destructive / genuinely-open parts

1. **The 101 spam tickets.** The old miner already spawned 101+ `[feedback-loop] Investigate transcript-mined rule confusion…` tickets into root [.ticket](.ticket) in a 22-min window. Your decision #4 said "investigate why each was triggered." My read: they're uniformly false positives (keyword overlap against committed transcripts). **Bulk-cancel them** (with a reason, preserving history), or want a sampled investigation first?

2. **171 tracked transcript files.** The gitignore fix stops *future* leaks, but 57 session folders are already committed. Want me to `git rm --cached` them (keeps on disk, evicts from the index)? That's a mass git op on committed files — confirming before I touch it.

3. **Explicit-ingestion mining + failed-tool→entity mapping (decisions #1/#4).** I intentionally did *not* guess the feedback-ingestion tool-name schema or what entity a failed tool call maps to — both are genuinely open. Want me to ground these in the actual `feedback-mcp`/`feedback-cli` tool definitions and bring you a concrete design (including the backtraceable/verifiable ticket format) before wiring auto-actions?

4. **The pre-existing test-cli break** — fix the 5 provenance fixtures as a separate small commit so the crate goes green, or leave it for the interoperability-contract owner?

My recommendation: **1 = bulk-cancel**, **2 = yes untrack**, **3 = yes, design-first**, **4 = yes, fix it** (it's mechanical and unblocks the crate). Say the word and I'll proceed in that order.

events.reasoning_text:
The spam cleanup worked—598 legitimate ticket folders remain in the store, which suggests the original collection had around 1,120 tickets before I removed the 522 spam entries. That's a substantial number but reasonable for a large project.

I should verify the index is consistent since each delete command reindexed automatically, so a forced scan probably isn't necessary. Now I'm moving to decision #3: creating granular tickets for the remaining design work and updating the main ticket with current status. I'm planning to create several new tickets in the memory-api workspace to capture the deferred design-heavy tasks, starting with explicit feedback-ingestion mining from structured tool calls.

I need to define how to parse tool arguments for target URN, rating, and notes, then record backtraceable feedback entries with proper provenance. I also need to investigate how failed tool calls map to entities and whether they should be recorded as feedback signals. Then I'll implement a breadth-first entity queue for deterministic signal detection, and finally establish a ticket template for feedback-derived follow-ups that's backtraceable, actionable, and verifiable.

The key dependencies are that provenance fields (session ID, turn sequence, tool call ID) are foundational—they're needed before I can implement explicit ingestion and the ticket format. The failed-tool mapping is somewhat independent but feeds into the ticket format work. The BFS queue depends on having entities to mine from the ingestion and mapping steps. All of this ties back to tracker 5d4078.

I'm planning to create these as improvement tickets in the memory-api workspace with rich descriptions and dependency edges linking them together. I'll use the MCP create_ticket calls for each one to capture detailed descriptions, then wire up the depends_on relationships between them. T1 should detect explicit feedback-ingestion tool calls from the session metadata, parse the tool arguments for entity URN, rating, and notes, then record a backtraceable FeedbackEntry with provenance including session ID and tool call ID.

For T2, I need to investigate what failed tool calls should map to—whether it's the tool identity itself, an entity referenced in the tool arguments, or nothing at all—by examining the session transcripts from those 522 previously auto-mined tickets, then document a deterministic mapping policy without relying on message-text heuristics.

For T3, I'm designing a breadth-first entity queue that processes turns sequentially, enqueues newly-discovered entity URNs with deduplication, and mines only those queued entities—or alternatively, mines only entities detected upfront if that's simpler to document and test deterministically.

For T4, I'm extending FeedbackProvenance to include optional turn_sequence and tool_call_id fields so feedback entries can trace back through the session history and identify which specific tool invocation triggered them.

For T5, I'm defining a follow-up ticket template that links back to the originating session, turn, tool call, and FeedbackEntry, then re-enabling synthesis now that we have proper traceability to reduce false positives.

Now I'm mapping out the dependency graph: T1 blocks T5, T3, and the main tracker; T5 also depends on T2 and T4; and the activation ticket hinges on T1 and T5 being complete. ct — it can't close until the ring is truly activated. So I'll set 6b0002bf to depend on all five new tickets. Now I need to create these tickets in the memory-api workspace and set up those dependencies.

arguments.title: [feedback-api][provenance] Extend FeedbackProvenance with session/turn/tool-call backtrace refs
arguments.description:
## Motivation
`FeedbackProvenance` currently carries only `session_id`, `author`, `executed_at` (crates/feedback-api/src/lib.rs). The removed transcript miner even dropped `session_id` entirely, so mined entries were not backtraceable to their origin. Backtraceability is a hard requirement for the robust feedback model.

## Scope
- Add optional `turn_sequence: Option<usize>` and `tool_call_id: Option<String>` to `FeedbackProvenance` (and confirm `session_id` is always populated for mined/ingested entries).
- Bump `FEEDBACK_SCHEMA_VERSION` if the on-disk shape changes; keep deserialization backward compatible.
- Provide a constructor path that threads these refs from a captured `SessionTurn`.

## Non-goals
- No message-text analysis. Structured metadata only.

## Acceptance criteria
- serde round-trip test for the extended provenance.
- A mined/ingested entry asserts populated `session_id` + `turn_sequence` + `tool_call_id`.

## Foundational for
- Explicit feedback-ingestion mining.
- Re-enabling backtraceable follow-up ticket synthesis.

arguments.title: [feedback-api][ingestion] Mine explicit feedback-ingestion tool calls from structured session metadata
arguments.description:
## Motivation
The highest-fidelity feedback signal is an explicit feedback-ingestion tool call the agent already made during a session. It carries an unambiguous target entity + rating + note in structured arguments — no inference required.

## Scope
- Add an `ExplicitIngestion` variant to the structured signal model in `crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs`.
- Ground the exact captured tool-name schema against the real `feedback-mcp` / `feedback-cli` / `feedback-http` definitions — DO NOT guess the tool name. Confirm how the Copilot capture pipeline records the tool name in `SessionTurn.tool_name` / `event_meta`.
- Parse `event_meta.tool_arguments_json` for target `EntityUrn` + `FeedbackRating` + note + `FeedbackNoteKind`.
- Record a backtraceable `FeedbackEntry` (source = Agent or User) via `EntityFeedbackStore`, with provenance carrying session/turn/tool-call refs.

## Non-goals
- No free-text / keyword parsing of message content.
- No auto-ticket creation here (that is the follow-up-format ticket).

## Dependencies
- Requires the extended `FeedbackProvenance` backtrace refs.

## Acceptance criteria
- Unit test: a synthetic tool turn with feedback-ingest args produces exactly one recorded `FeedbackEntry` with correct target/rating and populated provenance.
- No code path inspects `SessionTurn.content` text.

arguments.title: [feedback-api][signals] Define failed-tool-call to entity mapping and recording policy
arguments.description:
## Motivation
`mine_structured_feedback_signals` now detects `FailedToolCall` signals (`event_meta.tool_success == Some(false)`), but a failed tool call has no inherent target entity. We must decide, with evidence, what (if anything) it maps to before recording feedback or synthesizing tickets from it.

## Scope
- Investigate the triggers of the 522 previously auto-mined (now bulk-deleted) tickets using the committed `.session/sessions/*` transcripts as evidence — understand what patterns fired and whether any findings are genuinely useful.
- Decide the mapping for a failed tool call: tool identity, an argument-referenced entity (ticket/spec/rule id extracted from `tool_arguments_json`), or no entity (signal-only, not feedback).
- Produce a typed representation + documented policy. Encode error/ambiguity states in the type system (no silent fallbacks).

## Non-goals
- No message-text heuristics.

## Acceptance criteria
- A written policy (in the ticket/spec) for failed-tool-call handling.
- Typed model + unit test covering the confident cases and an explicit "unmapped" outcome.

data_json.content:
Spam fully purged: **0 remaining**, 598 legitimate tickets intact, 0 delete failures. Decision #1 done.

Now **decision #3: the granular ticket workflow** for the remaining design-heavy work. Creating 5 scoped tickets in the `memory-api` workspace (where the feedback code lives).

- Record a backtraceable `FeedbackEntry` (source = Agent or User) via `EntityFeedbackStore`, with provenance carrying session/turn/tool-call refs.

## Non-goals
- No free-text / keyword parsing of message content.
- No auto-ticket creation here (that is the follow-up-format ticket).

## Dependencies
- Requires the extended `FeedbackProvenance` backtrace refs.

## Acceptance criteria
- Unit test: a synthetic tool turn with feedback-ingest args produces exactly one recorded `FeedbackEntry` with correct target/rating and populated provenance.
- No code path inspects `SessionTurn.content` text.

arguments.title: [feedback-api][signals] Define failed-tool-call to entity mapping and recording policy
arguments.description:
## Motivation
`mine_structured_feedback_signals` now detects `FailedToolCall` signals (`event_meta.tool_success == Some(false)`), but a failed tool call has no inherent target entity. We must decide, with evidence, what (if anything) it maps to before recording feedback or synthesizing tickets from it.

## Scope
- Investigate the triggers of the 522 previously auto-mined (now bulk-deleted) tickets using the committed `.session/sessions/*` transcripts as evidence — understand what patterns fired and whether any findings are genuinely useful.
- Decide the mapping for a failed tool call: tool identity, an argument-referenced entity (ticket/spec/rule id extracted from `tool_arguments_json`), or no entity (signal-only, not feedback).
- Produce a typed representation + documented policy. Encode error/ambiguity states in the type system (no silent fallbacks).

## Non-goals
- No message-text heuristics.

## Acceptance criteria
- A written policy (in the ticket/spec) for failed-tool-call handling.
- Typed model + unit test covering the confident cases and an explicit "unmapped" outcome.

data_json.reasoningText:
The spam cleanup worked—598 legitimate ticket folders remain in the store, which suggests the original collection had around 1,120 tickets before I removed the 522 spam entries. That's a substantial number but reasonable for a large project.

I should verify the index is consistent since each delete command reindexed automatically, so a forced scan probably isn't necessary. Now I'm moving to decision #3: creating granular tickets for the remaining design work and updating the main ticket with current status. I'm planning to create several new tickets in the memory-api workspace to capture the deferred design-heavy tasks, starting with explicit feedback-ingestion mining from structured tool calls.

I need to define how to parse tool arguments for target URN, rating, and notes, then record backtraceable feedback entries with proper provenance. I also need to investigate how failed tool calls map to entities and whether they should be recorded as feedback signals. Then I'll implement a breadth-first entity queue for deterministic signal detection, and finally establish a ticket template for feedback-derived follow-ups that's backtraceable, actionable, and verifiable.

The key dependencies are that provenance fields (session ID, turn sequence, tool call ID) are foundational—they're needed before I can implement explicit ingestion and the ticket format. The failed-tool mapping is somewhat independent but feeds into the ticket format work. The BFS queue depends on having entities to mine from the ingestion and mapping steps. All of this ties back to tracker 5d4078.

I'm planning to create these as improvement tickets in the memory-api workspace with rich descriptions and dependency edges linking them together. I'll use the MCP create_ticket calls for each one to capture detailed descriptions, then wire up the depends_on relationships between them. T1 should detect explicit feedback-ingestion tool calls from the session metadata, parse the tool arguments for entity URN, rating, and notes, then record a backtraceable FeedbackEntry with provenance including session ID and tool call ID.

For T2, I need to investigate what failed tool calls should map to—whether it's the tool identity itself, an entity referenced in the tool arguments, or nothing at all—by examining the session transcripts from those 522 previously auto-mined tickets, then document a deterministic mapping policy without relying on message-text heuristics.

For T3, I'm designing a breadth-first entity queue that processes turns sequentially, enqueues newly-discovered entity URNs with deduplication, and mines only those queued entities—or alternatively, mines only entities detected upfront if that's simpler to document and test deterministically.

For T4, I'm extending FeedbackProvenance to include optional turn_sequence and tool_call_id fields so feedback entries can trace back through the session history and identify which specific tool invocation triggered them.

For T5, I'm defining a follow-up ticket template that links back to the originating session, turn, tool call, and FeedbackEntry, then re-enabling synthesis now that we have proper traceability to reduce false positives.

Now I'm mapping out the dependency graph: T1 blocks T5, T3, and the main tracker; T5 also depends on T2 and T4; and the activation ticket hinges on T1 and T5 being complete. ct — it can't close until the ring is truly activated. So I'll set 6b0002bf to depend on all five new tickets. Now I need to create these tickets in the memory-api workspace and set up those dependencies.

data.content:
Spam fully purged: **0 remaining**, 598 legitimate tickets intact, 0 delete failures. Decision #1 done.

Now **decision #3: the granular ticket workflow** for the remaining design-heavy work. Creating 5 scoped tickets in the `memory-api` workspace (where the feedback code lives).

arguments.title: [feedback-api][provenance] Extend FeedbackProvenance with session/turn/tool-call backtrace refs
arguments.description:
## Motivation
`FeedbackProvenance` currently carries only `session_id`, `author`, `executed_at` (crates/feedback-api/src/lib.rs). The removed transcript miner even dropped `session_id` entirely, so mined entries were not backtraceable to their origin. Backtraceability is a hard requirement for the robust feedback model.

## Scope
- Add optional `turn_sequence: Option<usize>` and `tool_call_id: Option<String>` to `FeedbackProvenance` (and confirm `session_id` is always populated for mined/ingested entries).
- Bump `FEEDBACK_SCHEMA_VERSION` if the on-disk shape changes; keep deserialization backward compatible.
- Provide a constructor path that threads these refs from a captured `SessionTurn`.

## Non-goals
- No message-text analysis. Structured metadata only.

## Acceptance criteria
- serde round-trip test for the extended provenance.
- A mined/ingested entry asserts populated `session_id` + `turn_sequence` + `tool_call_id`.

## Foundational for
- Explicit feedback-ingestion mining.
- Re-enabling backtraceable follow-up ticket synthesis.

- Typed model + unit test covering the confident cases and an explicit "unmapped" outcome.

arguments.title: [feedback-api][signals] Define failed-tool-call to entity mapping and recording policy
arguments.description:
## Motivation
`mine_structured_feedback_signals` now detects `FailedToolCall` signals (`event_meta.tool_success == Some(false)`), but a failed tool call has no inherent target entity. We must decide, with evidence, what (if anything) it maps to before recording feedback or synthesizing tickets from it.

## Scope
- Investigate the triggers of the 522 previously auto-mined (now bulk-deleted) tickets using the committed `.session/sessions/*` transcripts as evidence — understand what patterns fired and whether any findings are genuinely useful.
- Decide the mapping for a failed tool call: tool identity, an argument-referenced entity (ticket/spec/rule id extracted from `tool_arguments_json`), or no entity (signal-only, not feedback).
- Produce a typed representation + documented policy. Encode error/ambiguity states in the type system (no silent fallbacks).

## Non-goals
- No message-text heuristics.

## Acceptance criteria
- A written policy (in the ticket/spec) for failed-tool-call handling.
- Typed model + unit test covering the confident cases and an explicit "unmapped" outcome.

tool_arguments_json.title: [feedback-api][provenance] Extend FeedbackProvenance with session/turn/tool-call backtrace refs
tool_arguments_json.description:
## Motivation
`FeedbackProvenance` currently carries only `session_id`, `author`, `executed_at` (crates/feedback-api/src/lib.rs). The removed transcript miner even dropped `session_id` entirely, so mined entries were not backtraceable to their origin. Backtraceability is a hard requirement for the robust feedback model.

## Scope
- Add optional `turn_sequence: Option<usize>` and `tool_call_id: Option<String>` to `FeedbackProvenance` (and confirm `session_id` is always populated for mined/ingested entries).
- Bump `FEEDBACK_SCHEMA_VERSION` if the on-disk shape changes; keep deserialization backward compatible.
- Provide a constructor path that threads these refs from a captured `SessionTurn`.

## Non-goals
- No message-text analysis. Structured metadata only.

## Acceptance criteria
- serde round-trip test for the extended provenance.
- A mined/ingested entry asserts populated `session_id` + `turn_sequence` + `tool_call_id`.

## Foundational for
- Explicit feedback-ingestion mining.
- Re-enabling backtraceable follow-up ticket synthesis.

events.reasoning_text: I've created three task IDs for the provenance tracking, ingestion process, and failed-tool mapping, and now I need to set up the BFS queue structure along with the ticket format before re-enabling the system.

arguments.description:
## Motivation
Structured feedback mining must iterate discovered entities deterministically. Per the agreed design (decision #2): iterate breadth-first, and only queue newly-discovered entities for detection after each unit is processed, so entities discovered later are appended to the queue.

## Scope
- Implement a BFS work-queue in the structured miner: process signals/turns in order, enqueue newly-discovered entity URNs, dedupe by URN, and mine only queued entities.
- Acceptable alternative (document the choice if taken): mine only the entities detected at the beginning.
- Deterministic, reproducible ordering.

## Dependencies
- Requires entity discovery from explicit-ingestion mining and the failed-tool-call mapping policy.

## Non-goals
- No message-text heuristics.

## Acceptance criteria
- A test asserting deterministic BFS ordering and correct dedupe for a multi-entity session.
arguments.title: [feedback-api][signals] Deterministic BFS entity queue for structured feedback mining

arguments.description:
## Motivation
The stop-hook (`crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs`) currently only logs structured signals — auto-ticket synthesis was paused after the naive miner created 522 false-positive tickets (bulk-deleted). Before re-enabling, we must define a follow-up ticket format that is backtraceable, forward-actionable, and verifiable.

## Scope
- Define the follow-up ticket template so feedback-derived tickets are:
  - **backtraceable** — link the originating session/turn/tool-call and the `FeedbackEntry`;
  - **forward-actionable** — explicit next step + owning component;
  - **verifiable** — linked validation spec / acceptance criteria.
- Re-enable synthesis in the stop-hook, gated on confident structured signals only (explicit ingestion and/or the agreed failed-tool policy).
- Replace title-string-equality dedupe with robust idempotent dedupe (stable key from signal + entity + session).

## Dependencies
- Explicit feedback-ingestion mining, failed-tool-call mapping policy, and extended provenance.

## Non-goals
- No free-text mining. No unbounded ticket creation.

## Acceptance criteria
- A controlled session produces exactly one well-formed, backtraceable follow-up ticket.
- Re-running the same session produces no duplicate.
- The generated ticket links session/turn/tool-call + `FeedbackEntry` + a validation reference.
arguments.title: [feedback-api][analyzer] Backtraceable, verifiable follow-up ticket format; re-enable synthesis behind it

data_json.content: Three created. Now the remaining two (BFS queue + follow-up ticket format).

events.reasoning_text:
All five tickets have been successfully created with their respective IDs assigned for provenance tracking, data ingestion, tool mapping failures, queue management, and ticket formatting.

Now I'm setting up the dependency graph by establishing edges between these tickets. T1 depends on T4 since ingestion needs provenance data first, T5 (synthesis) depends on T1, T2, and T4 to pull everything together, T3 (BFS queue) depends on T1 and T2 for its traversal logic, and the activation ticket depends on all five core tickets since the ring can't be activated until everything is working properly. I'm adding all 11 dependency edges in parallel through the memory API.

data_json.content: All 5 tickets created. Wiring the dependency graph: `T1→T4`, `T5→{T1,T2,T4}`, `T3→{T1,T2}`, and the activation ticket `6b0002bf` depends on all five (it can't close until the ring genuinely works).

events.reasoning_text:
All the edges are connected now, so the dependency graph is complete. I'm checking that the frontier ordering is correct—T4 should surface as the first actionable ticket since it has no dependencies within this new set. arguments.author: copilot-opus
arguments.description:
## Goal
Make the feedback system actually usable and self-driving. The feedback-api crate, schema, transports, and redistributed ring edges exist and compile, but the ring has never fired: 3 of 4 edges have no production caller, the MCP transport is registered nowhere, and agents are still routed to the old rule-feedback surface. This ticket wires the three missing layers so signals are collected, discoverable, analyzed, and synthesized into actions.

## Problem (evidence, 2026-07-12)
- `recompute_spec_verified_state` (spec-api/src/verification.rs), `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion` (session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs), and `ingest_frontend_feedback` (feedback-api/src/frontend.rs) have ZERO production callers — only re-exports + `#[test]` references.
- Only the missing-rule edge fires, via rule-cli dispatch (`emit_missing_rule_match_signal` -> ticket-api orchestration).
- `feedback-mcp` is registered in no MCP client config; `feedback-cli`/`feedback-http` likewise undiscoverable.
- AGENTS.md Feedback Workflow still points at `rule feedback` / `rule_record_feedback` (old rule-entry feedback), not the feedback-api surface.
- Analysis primitives exist (`low_rated_entities`, `unresolved_note_entities`, `summary_for`) but nothing aggregates them or issues actions.

## Scope
### 1. Discovery wiring
- Rewrite the AGENTS.md Feedback Workflow section to document the feedback-api surface: how to `feedback ingest` a rating/note on any entity URN (rule/spec/ticket), when to run `rule missing-rule`, and how to read `feedback summary`/`inbox`.
- Register `feedback-mcp` in the repo MCP client config so `feedback_ingest`/`feedback_inbox`/`feedback_query`/`feedback_summary`/`feedback_mine` are reachable.
- Add a concise `.agents/instructions/feedback.instructions.md` (or rule entry) encouraging agents to record feedback that improves the codebase.

### 2. Collection wiring
- Call `ingest_frontend_feedback` from the viewer frontends (user + web feedback edge).
- Add a session Stop-hook that runs `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion` over the just-ended transcript and persists results.

### 3. Analyzer loop (synthesize actions)
- A scheduled/handoff-time analyzer reads `low_rated_entities` + `unresolved_note_entities` and issues actions: file improvement tickets for low-rated rules/specs, and invoke `recompute_spec_verified_state` when a validation execution lands.

## Acceptance criteria
- At least one live end-to-end firing per ring edge (execution->verified, transcript-mining, frontend-ingest, missing-rule), invoked from a registered transport or hook (not a unit test), each with a recorded .test execution.
- `feedback-mcp` reachable from an agent MCP client; AGENTS.md Feedback Workflow references the feedback-api surface, not rule-feedback.
- Analyzer produces at least one synthesized action (ticket or spec recompute) from stored low-rated/unresolved signals, with evidence.
- No dependency cycle introduced; feedback-api remains a pure sink.

## Session progress — 2026-07-13 (robust-model rework)
Discovery/collection/analyzer wiring landed earlier in the track; this session hardened the model after review found the miner was a false-positive factory.

Landed + validated this session:
- Removed free-text transcript mining (tokenizer + "confusion markers" + `overlap < 2`) that had spam-created **522** false-positive `[feedback-loop]` tickets (now bulk-deleted). Replaced with structured-metadata-only detection in `crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs`: `mine_structured_feedback_signals` detects failed tool calls via `event_meta.tool_success == Some(false)` and returns backtraceable `StructuredFeedbackSignal` (tool_call_id/event_id). No `SessionTurn.content` inspection.
- Stop-hook (`crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs`) no longer auto-creates tickets — it logs a structured signal summary. Auto-synthesis is PAUSED pending a backtraceable/verifiable ticket format.
- Typed spec verification: `recompute_spec_verified_state` returns `SpecVerificationOutcome` (`NoGuards` / `Pending{missing_guards}` / `Failed{failed_guards}` / `Verified`) instead of the ambiguous collapsed `Ok(false)`. `test-cli` now emits structured `spec_verification` reports instead of swallowing errors into a `Vec<String>`.
- Fixed 5 pre-existing `test-cli` fixture failures caused by the committed interoperability provenance contract (commit 745d2ef) — added `domain`/`operation`/`run_id`/links.
- Kept `.session` transcripts tracked in git per decision (reverted an interim gitignore change).

Validation: session-api 13/13, spec-api verification 3/3 (2 new typed-outcome tests), test-cli 8/8. Evidence: test-api execution `exec-vt-feedback-ring-robust-model-20260713` (passed), spec `vt-feedback-ring-robust-model`.

Remaining ring-activation work is decomposed into dependency-linked children (this ticket now `depends_on` all five):
- `a7601cb7` — provenance backtrace refs (foundational)
- `b4954d6c` — explicit feedback-ingestion mining (depends on provenance)
- `16e112a7` — failed-tool-call → entity mapping policy
- `3fa60398` — BFS entity queue (depends on ingestion + mapping)
- `3d4c4739` — backtraceable/verifiable follow-up ticket format + re-enable synthesis (depends on ingestion + mapping + provenance)

data_json.content: The dependency graph is wired (frontier will surface the provenance ticket first — it has no prerequisites). Let me append an accurate progress section to the activation ticket, preserving its original diagnosis.

- Add a concise `.agents/instructions/feedback.instructions.md` (or rule entry) encouraging agents to record feedback that improves the codebase.

### 2. Collection wiring
- Call `ingest_frontend_feedback` from the viewer frontends (user + web feedback edge).
- Add a session Stop-hook that runs `mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion` over the just-ended transcript and persists results.

### 3. Analyzer loop (synthesize actions)
- A scheduled/handoff-time analyzer reads `low_rated_entities` + `unresolved_note_entities` and issues actions: file improvement tickets for low-rated rules/specs, and invoke `recompute_spec_verified_state` when a validation execution lands.

## Acceptance criteria
- At least one live end-to-end firing per ring edge (execution->verified, transcript-mining, frontend-ingest, missing-rule), invoked from a registered transport or hook (not a unit test), each with a recorded .test execution.
- `feedback-mcp` reachable from an agent MCP client; AGENTS.md Feedback Workflow references the feedback-api surface, not rule-feedback.
- Analyzer produces at least one synthesized action (ticket or spec recompute) from stored low-rated/unresolved signals, with evidence.
- No dependency cycle introduced; feedback-api remains a pure sink.

## Session progress — 2026-07-13 (robust-model rework)
Discovery/collection/analyzer wiring landed earlier in the track; this session hardened the model after review found the miner was a false-positive factory.

Landed + validated this session:
- Removed free-text transcript mining (tokenizer + "confusion markers" + `overlap < 2`) that had spam-created **522** false-positive `[feedback-loop]` tickets (now bulk-deleted). Replaced with structured-metadata-only detection in `crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs`: `mine_structured_feedback_signals` detects failed tool calls via `event_meta.tool_success == Some(false)` and returns backtraceable `StructuredFeedbackSignal` (tool_call_id/event_id). No `SessionTurn.content` inspection.
- Stop-hook (`crates/session-api/src/bin/copilot-capture-hook.rs`) no longer auto-creates tickets — it logs a structured signal summary. Auto-synthesis is PAUSED pending a backtraceable/verifiable ticket format.
- Typed spec verification: `recompute_spec_verified_state` returns `SpecVerificationOutcome` (`NoGuards` / `Pending{missing_guards}` / `Failed{failed_guards}` / `Verified`) instead of the ambiguous collapsed `Ok(false)`. `test-cli` now emits structured `spec_verification` reports instead of swallowing errors into a `Vec<String>`.
- Fixed 5 pre-existing `test-cli` fixture failures caused by the committed interoperability provenance contract (commit 745d2ef) — added `domain`/`operation`/`run_id`/links.
- Kept `.session` transcripts tracked in git per decision (reverted an interim gitignore change).

Validation: session-api 13/13, spec-api verification 3/3 (2 new typed-outcome tests), test-cli 8/8. Evidence: test-api execution `exec-vt-feedback-ring-robust-model-20260713` (passed), spec `vt-feedback-ring-robust-model`.

Remaining ring-activation work is decomposed into dependency-linked children (this ticket now `depends_on` all five):
- `a7601cb7` — provenance backtrace refs (foundational)
- `b4954d6c` — explicit feedback-ingestion mining (depends on provenance)
- `16e112a7` — failed-tool-call → entity mapping policy
- `3fa60398` — BFS entity queue (depends on ingestion + mapping)
- `3d4c4739` — backtraceable/verifiable follow-up ticket format + re-enable synthesis (depends on ingestion + mapping + provenance)

arguments.author: copilot-opus
arguments.description:
## Goal
Make the feedback system actually usable and self-driving. The feedback-api crate, schema, transports, and redistributed ring edges exist and compile, but the ring has never fired: 3 of 4 edges have no production caller, the MCP transport is registered nowhere, and agents are still routed to the old rule-feedback surface. - Only the missing-rule edge fires, via rule-cli dispatch (`emit_missing_rule_match_signal` -> ticket-api orchestration).
- `feedback-mcp` is registered in no MCP client config; `feedback-cli`/`feedback-http` likewise undiscoverable.
- AGENTS.md Feedback Workflow still points at `rule feedback` / `rule_record_feedback` (old rule-entry feedback), not the feedback-api surface.

>
> ## Next actions, in dependency order
> 1. [a7601cb7 Extend FeedbackProvenance with session/turn/tool-call refs](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a7601cb7-6c92-4891-aa55-07ab46125bb8/ticket.toml) — foundational, start here (no prerequisites).
