data_json.content:
Here is the standard-format handoff prompt.

~~~text
# Handoff — Feedback ring: structured mining + backtraceable synthesis

## Overview
Resume the feedback-ring activation track from [./](./). The prior session hardened the feedback model after review found the transcript miner was a false-positive factory that had spam-created 522 auto-tickets. The naive free-text miner is now removed and replaced with a structured-metadata-only detector; spec verified-state recomputation is now a typed outcome; the stop-hook's auto-ticket synthesis is intentionally paused; 522 spam tickets were bulk-deleted; and the remaining ring-activation work is decomposed into five dependency-linked tickets. Your job is to execute those five tickets in dependency order to re-activate the ring on a robust, backtraceable, verifiable basis using structured signals only.

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend
- `<workspace>` = the ticket/test workspace path `c:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api` (pass as `workspace` to ticket-mcp / test-mcp; ticket work lives here).
- `ACT` = activation ticket (see Ticket Legend).
- `TRACKER` = broader ring tracker (see Ticket Legend).
- `PROV`, `INGEST`, `MAP`, `BFS`, `SYNTH` = the five child tickets (see Ticket Legend).

## Ticket Legend (shorthand → full UUID + canonical title)
- `ACT` = `6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc` — [6b0002bf [feedback-api][activation] Wire feedback discovery, collection, and analyzer loop (ring activation)](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc/ticket.toml)
- `TRACKER` = `5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d` — [5d4078fa [content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring — execution→verified, transcript mining, missing-rule tickets, ticket-entity feedback](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/ticket.toml)
- `PROV` = `a7601cb7-6c92-4891-aa55-07ab46125bb8` — [a7601cb7 [feedback-api][provenance] Extend FeedbackProvenance with session/turn/tool-call backtrace refs](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a7601cb7-6c92-4891-aa55-07ab46125bb8/ticket.toml)
- `INGEST` = `b4954d6c-0451-49ed-8939-11f6568558f5` — [b4954d6c [feedback-api][ingestion] Mine explicit feedback-ingestion tool calls from structured session metadata](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b4954d6c-0451-49ed-8939-11f6568558f5/ticket.toml)
- `MAP` = `16e112a7-5ab0-45a5-87c8-7d89d07ffd16` — [16e112a7 [feedback-api][signals] Define failed-tool-call to entity mapping and recording policy](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/16e112a7-5ab0-45a5-87c8-7d89d07ffd16/ticket.toml)
- `BFS` = `3fa60398-3154-46ee-aca5-8d87541bac1e` — [3fa60398 [feedback-api][signals] Deterministic BFS entity queue for structured feedback mining](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/3fa60398-3154-46ee-aca5-8d87541bac1e/ticket.toml)
- `SYNTH` = `3d4c4739-3138-40be-947d-556e5f7de53a` — [3d4c4739 [feedback-api][analyzer] Backtraceable, verifiable follow-up ticket format; re-enable synthesis behind it](./memory-api/.ticket/tickets/3d4c4739-3138-40be-947d-556e5f7de53a/ticket.toml)

## Long-Horizon Goal (epic)
Make the feedback ring genuinely self-driving from **structured signals only** (no message-text heuristics until embedding-based context reasoning lands), where every mined entry and every synthesized ticket is backtraceable to its origin turn/tool-call and is independently verifiable. Epic ticket: `ACT` (`6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc`), which now `depends_on` all five child tickets and cannot close until the ring fires end-to-end with recorded evidence.

## Findings And Decisions
- The prior miner detected "rule confusion" from free-text (tokenizer + confusion-marker prefixes + `overlap < 2`) and auto-created tickets. Run against committed `.session` transcripts (full of "error/fail/conflict") it produced 522 false-positive tickets. Decision: mine **structured metadata only**; defer content reasoning to a future embedding model.
- `SessionTurnEventMeta` already carries the right structured signals (`tool_success`, `tool_call_id`, `tool_arguments_json`) in [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/model.rs) — the detector needs no NLP.
- Decision: entity iteration must be deterministic breadth-first, appending newly-discovered entities to a queue after each unit (or, acceptable alternative, mine only entities detected at the beginning).
- Decision: only ship logic that confidently works with actionable error states encoded in the type system (drove the typed `SpecVerificationOutcome` and the "no guessing the ingestion tool-name schema" constraint).
- Decision: `.session` transcript files stay tracked in git (an interim `.gitignore` exclusion was reverted).
- Decision: the 522 mined tickets were bulk-deleted (not salvaged); investigate trigger patterns from transcripts under `MAP`.

## What Already Landed (validated — do not redo)
- Structured detector `mine_structured_feedback_signals` in [./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs](./memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs): detects failed tool calls via `event_meta.tool_success == Some(false)`, returns backtraceable `StructuredFeedbackSignal`; no `SessionTurn.content` inspection. Tests: 2 new, pass.

# Handoff — feedback-api Ring Activation: PROV/INGEST/MAP/BFS/SYNTH complete, awaiting review

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend

| Shorthand | Full ID | Canonical Title |
|---|---|---|
| ACT | 6b0002bf-c15b-4cc7-ac38-fbf66a07d1bc | [feedback-api][activation] Wire feedback discovery, collection, and analyzer loop (ring activation) |
| DESIGN | fb6aa078-1f8b-4a76-99bd-3a26190b1208 | [feedback-api][design] First-class feedback-api crate boundary and unified FeedbackEntry schema v1 |
| PROV | a7601cb7-6c92-4891-aa55-07ab46125bb8 | [feedback-api][provenance] Extend FeedbackProvenance with session/turn/tool-call backtrace refs |
| INGEST | b4954d6c-0451-49ed-8939-11f6568558f5 | [feedback-api][ingestion] Mine explicit feedback-ingestion tool calls from structured session metadata |
| MAP | 16e112a7-5ab0-45a5-87c8-7d89d07ffd16 | [feedback-api][signals] Define failed-tool-call to entity mapping and recording policy |
| BFS | 3fa60398-3154-46ee-aca5-8d87541bac1e | [feedback-api][signals] Deterministic BFS entity queue for structured feedback mining |
| SYNTH | 3d4c4739-3138-40be-947d-556e5f7de53a | [feedback-api][analyzer] Backtraceable, verifiable follow-up ticket format; re-enable synthesis behind it |

## Overview

This session re-activated the feedback ring in `memory-api`'s `feedback-api`/`session-api` crates: it extended `FeedbackProvenance` with session-turn/tool-call backtrace refs (PROV), added structured-signal-only mining for explicit feedback-ingestion tool calls (INGEST) and for failed tool calls with a grounded entity-mapping policy (MAP), built a deterministic deduped BFS entity-discovery queue (BFS), and re-enabled backtraceable/idempotent follow-up ticket synthesis in the Stop hook (SYNTH). DESIGN (a pre-existing blocker) was verified already complete. All 6 dependency tickets and their parent ACT sit in `in-review` — implementation, tests, and validation evidence are done; only reviewer close-out to `done` remains. **Do not close these tickets to `done`** — that is an explicit, repeated instruction from the user; leave them in `in-review` unless told otherwise.

## Key Findings And Decisions

- Real captured session transcripts (checked across 61 sessions / 2670 turns) have **zero** turns with `role: tool`. Tool-call/result data only exists in the separate `events.json` (`CopilotHookEvent` list, specifically `tool.execution_result` events). Any future mining work must read events, not turns, for tool-call data — this was the key architectural correction mid-session and is now baked into `mine_explicit_ingestion_signals` and `mine_failed_tool_call_signals`.

data_json.content:
Review complete. I have the full evidence picture. Here is my verdict — and per the standing instruction, **I have not closed any tickets** (that remains gated on explicit human approval).

## Code review — the 6 `in-review` tickets

### Verdict: implementation is genuinely strong; one real evidence-traceability gap to resolve before close-out.

**Independently verified as accurate:**
- **Tests pass** — I re-ran `cargo test -p feedback-api -p session-api` myself: **16 / 64 / 5 / 4 / 4, zero failures**.
- **States & graph** — all six are `in-review` on disk; edges intact (INGEST→PROV; BFS→{MAP,INGEST}; SYNTH→{MAP,PROV,INGEST}); DESIGN carries populated `implementation_summary`/`validation_summary`.
- **PROV** ([a7601cb7](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/a7601cb7-6c92-4891-aa55-07ab46125bb8/ticket.toml)) — [feedback-api/src/lib.rs](memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/lib.rs) schema v1→2, `turn_sequence`/`tool_call_id` + `from_session_turn`. ✔ matches scope.
- **INGEST/MAP/BFS** ([transcript_feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/transcript_feedback.rs)) — the standout is MAP: the failed-tool→entity policy is grounded in real data (115 failures / 5031 results / 61 sessions), fully typed (`UnmappedReason::{UnknownTool,NoEntityIdArgument,AmbiguousMultipleCandidates,NoSupportedEntityStore}`), and **never guesses**. The mid-session architectural correction (tool data lives in `CopilotHookEvent`s, not `role:tool` turns) is correctly baked in. ✔
- **SYNTH** ([follow_up.rs](memory-api/crates/session-api/src/follow_up.rs)) — synthesis gated to *successful* `ExplicitIngestion` + non-helpful rating; idempotent via UUIDv5(`session_id`+`tool_call_id`) with a `get_indexed` guard → `AlreadyExists`. This directly kills the original over-triggering failure mode. ✔

**Minor, non-blocking (by-design, documented in code):**
- Turn-based `mine_structured_feedback_signals` is dead on real data — but explicitly kept "for forward compatibility" in the hook.
- The synthesized ticket's `FeedbackEntry` backtrace is a cross-reference *instruction*, not a hard id link (today's `feedback_ingest` transport doesn't echo the entry id).

### ⚠️ The one finding that should block a clean close-out: evidence traceability

The handoff claims "linked test-mcp evidence" for the tickets, but:

1. **Test-store routing split (real bug).** `record_execution(workspace=…)` writes to `memory-api/.test` (17 execs), while `list_executions` has no workspace param and reads the **root** `.test` (15 execs). All feedback-ring evidence recorded to the nested store is **invisible** to the standard MCP query a reviewer/agent uses.
2. **Incomplete per-ticket evidence.** Even in the nested store, only **BFS** (`exec-vt-feedback-bfs-entity-queue-20260713`) and **SYNTH** (`exec-vt-feedback-followup-synthesis-20260713`) have linked executions. **PROV, INGEST, MAP have zero linked test-mcp executions in either store** — their validation rests solely on `cargo test`.

So the code and cargo-test validation are trustworthy, but the durable, ticket-linked `.test` evidence the DoD implies is partly missing and partly unreachable.

## Next-action status
- **Action 1 (review)** — ✅ **completed** (this report).
- **Action 2 (close to `done`)** — ⛔ **blocked**: requires explicit human approval (standing instruction) *and*, I'd recommend, resolving the evidence gap first: (a) decide/fix the `record`↔`list` test-store routing split so feedback-ring evidence is discoverable, and (b) record ticket-linked test-mcp executions for PROV/INGEST/MAP to the store `list_executions` actually reads.
- **Actions 3–4** — blocked behind Action 2.

I did not modify or close any tickets, and the board remains clean (no check-in needed). Want me to (1) record the three missing ticket-linked executions for PROV/INGEST/MAP, and/or (2) dig into the test-store routing split to make `memory-api/.test` evidence discoverable? Both are non-closing remediations I can do now; closing to `done` I'll leave for your explicit go-ahead.

data_json.content:
I've verified your critical finding against the actual code — it's real, and it's worse than a mere hardcoded constant. Here's the grounded verdict.

## The crime, confirmed

`prune_execution_runs` at [memory-api/crates/test-api/src/store.rs](memory-api/crates/test-api/src/store.rs#L470-L521) builds a *single* `newest_by_run: BTreeMap<String, DateTime>` keyed on **`run_id` alone** — no spec, no ticket, no namespace of any kind. Then it keeps the global top-2 and `fs::remove_file`s the rest. And [record_execution](memory-api/crates/test-api/src/store.rs#L133) fires it **unconditionally on every single write**.

So the "durable evidence store" is actually a garbage disposal with a 2-item memory that runs on every insert. You documented it as a landmine; it's really a self-resetting bear trap that re-arms itself after each write. Your report is accurate to the letter, including the part where it ate six *git-committed* DESIGN files. Chef's kiss of a bug.

## The detail your ticket missed (and it changes the fix)

There's an existing test at [store/tests.rs](memory-api/crates/test-api/src/store/tests.rs#L179-L215), `record_execution_keeps_only_newest_two_runs`, that **codifies this behavior as intended** — three runs, run1 expected deleted. That's the reason nobody caught this: the bug has a green test cheering it on. But note the detail: all three executions in that test share **one** `validation_spec_id` (`vt-a`).

That single fact makes **option 1 (scope pruning by `validation_spec_id`)** the clear winner over your options 2–4:

- It kills the cross-spec/cross-ticket data loss (PROV/INGEST/MAP/BFS/SYNTH/DESIGN are all *different* specs, so they'd stop competing for the same 2 slots).
- It keeps the existing churn-cap test meaningful (same spec → still keeps newest 2).
- It satisfies AC#1/#3 directly ("cannot cross ticket/spec boundaries") without inventing a new opt-in flag or config surface.
- AC#4 dissolves for free: your three-way `run_id` overhang is spread across different specs, so per-spec scoping leaves nothing pending.

Concretely: change the map key from `run_id` to `(validation_spec_id, run_id)`, compute the keep-window per spec, and gate deletion on the composite key. ~15 lines, plus a regression test that records 3 executions across 3 distinct specs with 3 distinct `run_id`s and asserts all three survive.

## What's blocking me

Two things stop me from just doing it right now:

1. I'm running in **Roast mode — explicitly read-only** ("you do not edit, execute, or fix anything"). Defusing this properly means editing `store.rs` and its tests.
2. There's no file-edit tool wired into this session, so the only mechanical path to the change would be terminal-driven edits — which I won't do without you asking.

## Verdict

Your finding is legit, your recovery was correct, and consolidating onto one `run_id` was the right emergency tourniquet. Highest-leverage fix: **scope `prune_execution_runs` by `validation_spec_id`** (ticket 1e8f6866 option 1) + a 3-spec regression test — not a keep-count bump or an opt-out flag, both of which leave the global blast radius intact.

Say the word (or drop me into an edit-capable mode) and I'll implement the scoped prune + regression test against 1e8f6866. Until then, your instinct is correct: **do not record another `.test` execution** — the trap is still armed with 3 runs against a keep-2 threshold.

Identified an existing test (record_execution_keeps_only_newest_two_runs) that codifies the buggy behavior but uses a single spec — making "scope pruning by validation_spec_id" (ticket option 1) the cleanest fix that preserves the churn-cap test while eliminating cross-ticket/spec deletion. There's an existing test at [store/tests.rs](memory-api/crates/test-api/src/store/tests.rs#L179-L215), `record_execution_keeps_only_newest_two_runs`, that **codifies this behavior as intended** — three runs, run1 expected deleted. That's the reason nobody caught this: the bug has a green test cheering it on. But note the detail: all three executions in that test share **one** `validation_spec_id` (`vt-a`).

That single fact makes **option 1 (scope pruning by `validation_spec_id`)** the clear winner over your options 2–4:

- It kills the cross-spec/cross-ticket data loss (PROV/INGEST/MAP/BFS/SYNTH/DESIGN are all *different* specs, so they'd stop competing for the same 2 slots).
- It keeps the existing churn-cap test meaningful (same spec → still keeps newest 2).
- It satisfies AC#1/#3 directly ("cannot cross ticket/spec boundaries") without inventing a new opt-in flag or config surface.
- AC#4 dissolves for free: your three-way `run_id` overhang is spread across different specs, so per-spec scoping leaves nothing pending.

Concretely: change the map key from `run_id` to `(validation_spec_id, run_id)`, compute the keep-window per spec, and gate deletion on the composite key. ~15 lines, plus a regression test that records 3 executions across 3 distinct specs with 3 distinct `run_id`s and asserts all three survive.

## What's blocking me

Two things stop me from just doing it right now:

1. I'm running in **Roast mode — explicitly read-only** ("you do not edit, execute, or fix anything"). Defusing this properly means editing `store.rs` and its tests.
2. There's no file-edit tool wired into this session, so the only mechanical path to the change would be terminal-driven edits — which I won't do without you asking.

## Verdict

Your finding is legit, your recovery was correct, and consolidating onto one `run_id` was the right emergency tourniquet. Highest-leverage fix: **scope `prune_execution_runs` by `validation_spec_id`** (ticket 1e8f6866 option 1) + a 3-spec regression test — not a keep-count bump or an opt-out flag, both of which leave the global blast radius intact.

Say the word (or drop me into an edit-capable mode) and I'll implement the scoped prune + regression test against 1e8f6866. Until then, your instinct is correct: **do not record another `.test` execution** — the trap is still armed with 3 runs against a keep-2 threshold.

arguments.newString:
Discovered and reproduced live during review of tickets a7601cb7 (PROV), b4954d6c (INGEST), 16e112a7 (MAP), 3fa60398 (BFS), 3d4c4739 (SYNTH) evidence recording. Related to 905d05ae (test-mcp record/list store routing split) but distinct: that ticket is about which store a read/write resolves to; this ticket is about writes to the *correct* store destroying *other* evidence already in it.

## Decision (2026-07-14 review) — chosen fix direction

Adopt **Option 1: scope pruning by `validation_spec_id`**. Rationale grounded in a fresh read of the code:

- `prune_execution_runs` (memory-api/crates/test-api/src/store.rs, ~L470-L521) currently keys retention on `run_id` alone, globally across the whole store, and `record_execution` (~L133) calls it unconditionally on every write. Change the retention key from `run_id` to the composite `(validation_spec_id, run_id)` and apply the keep-N window independently per spec.
- This eliminates the cross-ticket/cross-spec deletion (the real defect): the feedback-ring specs (PROV/INGEST/MAP/BFS/SYNTH plus DESIGN's specs) each occupy their own retention namespace, so recording one spec's execution can never delete another spec's evidence.
- It preserves the existing intentional churn-cap without touching that test: `record_execution_keeps_only_newest_two_runs` (memory-api/crates/test-api/src/store/tests.rs, ~L179-L215) records three runs of a *single* spec `vt-a`; per-spec scoping still keeps only the newest 2, so run1 is still pruned and the test stays green and meaningful.
- Rejected alternatives: opt-in flag (option 2) and keep-count bump (option 3) both leave the global blast radius intact; never-prune-ticket-linked (option 4) becomes redundant once scoping is per-spec.

Regression test to add (satisfies AC#2): record 3+ executions across 3 distinct `validation_spec_id`s with 3 distinct `run_id`s and assert all three survive `record_execution`.

AC#4 (the current 3-way `run_id` overhang) dissolves automatically once scoping is per-spec, because the overhanging runs belong to different specs and no longer compete for a shared global keep-2 budget.

## Decision (2026-07-14 review) — chosen fix direction

Adopt **Option 1: scope pruning by `validation_spec_id`**. Rationale grounded in a fresh read of the code:

- `prune_execution_runs` (memory-api/crates/test-api/src/store.rs, ~L470-L521) currently keys retention on `run_id` alone, globally across the whole store, and `record_execution` (~L133) calls it unconditionally on every write. Change the retention key from `run_id` to the composite `(validation_spec_id, run_id)` and apply the keep-N window independently per spec.
- This eliminates the cross-ticket/cross-spec deletion (the real defect): the feedback-ring specs (PROV/INGEST/MAP/BFS/SYNTH plus DESIGN's specs) each occupy their own retention namespace, so recording one spec's execution can never delete another spec's evidence.
- It preserves the existing intentional churn-cap without touching that test: `record_execution_keeps_only_newest_two_runs` (memory-api/crates/test-api/src/store/tests.rs, ~L179-L215) records three runs of a *single* spec `vt-a`; per-spec scoping still keeps only the newest 2, so run1 is still pruned and the test stays green and meaningful.
- Rejected alternatives: opt-in flag (option 2) and keep-count bump (option 3) both leave the global blast radius intact; never-prune-ticket-linked (option 4) becomes redundant once scoping is per-spec.

Regression test to add (satisfies AC#2): record 3+ executions across 3 distinct `validation_spec_id`s with 3 distinct `run_id`s and assert all three survive `record_execution`.

AC#4 (the current 3-way `run_id` overhang) dissolves automatically once scoping is per-spec, because the overhanging runs belong to different specs and no longer compete for a shared global keep-2 budget.

events.reasoning_text:
The Cargo.lock changes span multiple recent workstreams—feedback-ring, test-cli, rule-api, ticket-api, session-api, spec-api, and the feedback-http frontend in ticket-viewer—so while they're mixed, they all reflect current work. Since Cargo.lock represents the workspace's dependency graph, committing it is straightforward and low-risk.

For roast.agent.md, I'm realizing it's a generated file under .agents/agents/ that comes from the rule source 45-agents-agents.yaml, so hand-editing and committing it could cause the pre-commit hook to fail if rule files are staged. I should leave it out of this commit since it's not part of the feedback-ring track anyway and should be regenerated through rule sync instead.

Now looking at mcp.json, which adds the feedback-mcp server registration and is part of the feedback-ring work.

For the commit scope, I'm settling on including memory-api with all its feedback-ring and ticket store changes (committing inside it, then updating the pointer), plus Cargo.lock, .github/mcp.json, .session/, .feedback/, and the memory-api pointer at the root level. I'll exclude roast.agent.md since it's rule-generated, RESEARCH_VIEWER_ARCHITECTURE.md as a separate research track, and memory-viewers changes since that's the ticket-viewer frontend work.

The tricky part is that Cargo.lock is a workspace-level lockfile reflecting all members, including the ticket-viewer feedback-http dependency from memory-viewers, which is technically part of a different track.

Now I'm ready to start the actual commit. Inside memory-api, I need to verify it has the right git hooks configured and check whether pre-commit will run. Since I'm staging crates and store files but not rule files, the pre-commit hook should pass without triggering an unnecessary rule sync-check. The key thing is that memory-api/AGENTS.md isn't modified—only memory-viewers/AGENTS.md is—so that's clean.

I'll organize this into two focused commits: one for source files across the various crates, then another for the store files. Let me start by staging the source changes.

The source commit includes updates to feedback-api, session-api, spec-api, and the test tools. Before I push this, I should verify the build still works—though the prior session showed good test coverage and I've only modified ticket descriptions and edges, not the actual source code, so the prune fix remains unimplemented for now.
